title: Civil Rights Movement
description: Leaders, events, and legislation of the Civil Rights Movement

cards.back: Baptist minister and leader of the Civil Rights Movement; advocated nonviolent protest; delivered the "I Have a Dream" speech; assassinated in 1968

cards.front: Who delivered the "I Have a Dream" speech at the March on Washington in 1963?
cards.back: Martin Luther King Jr.
Open letter written by Martin Luther King Jr. while imprisoned; defended nonviolent civil disobedience and argued that people have a moral duty to disobey unjust laws

cards.front: Who wrote the Letter from Birmingham Jail?
cards.back: Martin Luther King Jr.
Civil rights activist who refused to give up her bus seat to a white passenger in Montgomery, Alabama, in 1955; her arrest sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott

cards.front: Whose arrest for refusing to give up her bus seat sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott?
cards.back: Rosa Parks
Civil rights leader and Nation of Islam spokesman who advocated Black self-determination and self-defense; later embraced a more inclusive vision after his pilgrimage to Mecca; assassinated in 1965

cards.front: Which civil rights leader was a spokesman for the Nation of Islam before later embracing a more inclusive vision of racial equality?
cards.back: Malcolm X
Civil rights leader, chairman of SNCC, and longtime U.S. congressman; led marchers across the Edmund Pettus Bridge on Bloody Sunday in Selma (1965)

cards.front: Which civil rights leader led marchers across the Edmund Pettus Bridge on Bloody Sunday?
cards.back: John Lewis
First Black justice on the U.S. Supreme Court (1967); as an NAACP lawyer, he argued and won Brown v. Board of Education (1954)

cards.front: Who was the first Black justice on the U.S. Supreme Court?
cards.back: Thurgood Marshall
NAACP field secretary in Mississippi who worked to overturn segregation; assassinated outside his home in 1963

cards.front: Which NAACP field secretary in Mississippi was assassinated outside his home in 1963?
cards.back: Medgar Evers
14-year-old African American boy from Chicago who was lynched in Mississippi in 1955; his mother's decision to hold an open-casket funeral helped galvanize the Civil Rights Movement

cards.front: Whose 1955 murder in Mississippi and open-casket funeral helped galvanize the Civil Rights Movement?
cards.back: Emmett Till
Scholar, activist, and co-founder of the NAACP (1909); wrote The Souls of Black Folk; advocated for full political and civil rights for African Americans

cards.front: Who co-founded the NAACP and wrote The Souls of Black Folk?
cards.back: W.E.B. Du Bois
Educator and leader who founded the Tuskegee Institute; advocated for Black economic self-reliance and vocational education; delivered the Atlanta Compromise speech (1895)

cards.front: Who founded the Tuskegee Institute and advocated for vocational education as the path to Black advancement?
cards.back: Booker T. Washington
Escaped slave who became a leading abolitionist, orator, and author; wrote Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass; advised President Lincoln during the Civil War

cards.front: Which escaped slave became a famous abolitionist orator and wrote a celebrated autobiography?
cards.back: Frederick Douglass
Escaped slave who led approximately 70 people to freedom via the Underground Railroad; known as the "Moses of her people"; also served as a Union spy during the Civil War

cards.front: Who was known as the "Moses of her people" for leading enslaved people to freedom on the Underground Railroad?
cards.back: Harriet Tubman
Abolitionist and women's rights activist born into slavery; famous for her "Ain't I a Woman?" speech delivered at a women's convention in 1851

cards.front: Who delivered the famous "Ain't I a Woman?" speech?
cards.back: Sojourner Truth
Labor and civil rights leader who organized the Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters; his threat of a march on Washington in 1941 led FDR to ban racial discrimination in defense industries

cards.front: Which labor leader organized the Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters and pressured FDR to ban discrimination in defense industries?
cards.back: A. Philip Randolph
SNCC chairman who popularized the phrase "Black Power" during a 1966 march in Mississippi; later changed his name to Kwame Ture

cards.front: Who popularized the phrase "Black Power" during a 1966 march?
cards.back: Stokely Carmichael
Voting rights activist from Mississippi; co-founded the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party; testified before the 1964 Democratic National Convention about voter suppression

cards.front: Which Mississippi activist co-founded the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party and testified at the 1964 Democratic National Convention?
cards.back: Fannie Lou Hamer
381-day boycott of city buses in Montgomery, Alabama, sparked by Rosa Parks's arrest; ended when the Supreme Court ruled bus segregation unconstitutional; launched MLK to national prominence

cards.front: What was the 381-day protest in Montgomery, Alabama, that ended bus segregation and launched MLK to national prominence?
cards.back: Montgomery Bus Boycott (1955–1956)
Nine Black students who integrated Central High School in Little Rock, Arkansas; President Eisenhower sent federal troops to enforce their enrollment

cards.front: Which president sent federal troops to Little Rock, Arkansas, to protect students integrating Central High School?
cards.back: Dwight D. Eisenhower
Four Black college students sat at a whites-only Woolworth's lunch counter in Greensboro, North Carolina; their peaceful protest inspired sit-ins across the South

cards.front: In which city did four college students stage a sit-in at a Woolworth's lunch counter in 1960, inspiring similar protests across the South?
cards.back: Greensboro, North Carolina
Interracial groups organized by CORE rode interstate buses into the segregated South to test Supreme Court rulings banning segregation in interstate travel; riders were met with violent resistance

cards.front: What were the 1961 interracial bus trips through the South that tested desegregation rulings called?
cards.back: Freedom Rides
Massive civil rights rally in Washington, D.C., where over 250,000 people gathered; Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech at the Lincoln Memorial

cards.front: At which 1963 event did Martin Luther King Jr. deliver his "I Have a Dream" speech?
cards.back: March on Washington
Ku Klux Klan bombing of a Black church in Birmingham, Alabama, that killed four young girls: Addie Mae Collins, Cynthia Wesley, Carole Robertson, and Carol Denise McNair

cards.front: What was the 1963 KKK attack in Birmingham, Alabama, that killed four young girls in a church?
cards.back: 16th Street Baptist Church bombing

Three marches for voting rights from Selma to Montgomery, Alabama; the first march on "Bloody Sunday" was met with police violence on the Edmund Pettus Bridge; helped lead to the Voting Rights Act of 1965

cards.front: What were the 1965 voting rights marches in Alabama that included "Bloody Sunday"?
cards.back: Selma to Montgomery marches

Federal law that banned discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin; outlawed segregation in public places and employment discrimination; signed by President Lyndon B. Johnson

cards.front: Which federal law banned racial discrimination in public places and employment, signed by LBJ in 1964?
cards.back: Civil Rights Act of 1964
Federal law that prohibited racial discrimination in voting; banned literacy tests and provided federal oversight of voter registration in areas with histories of discrimination

cards.front: Which 1965 law banned literacy tests and other barriers used to prevent African Americans from voting?
cards.back: Voting Rights Act of 1965
Abolished slavery and involuntary servitude in the United States

cards.front: Which amendment abolished slavery in the United States?
cards.back: 13th Amendment
Granted citizenship to all persons born in the United States and guaranteed equal protection under the law

cards.front: Which amendment guarantees equal protection under the law and granted citizenship to all persons born in the U.S.?
cards.back: 14th Amendment
Prohibited denying the right to vote based on race, color, or previous condition of servitude

cards.front: Which amendment prohibited denying the right to vote based on race?
cards.back: 15th Amendment
Supreme Court ruling that declared racial segregation in public schools unconstitutional, overturning Plessy v. Ferguson (1896); argued by Thurgood Marshall for the NAACP

cards.front: Which 1954 Supreme Court case declared school segregation unconstitutional?
cards.back: Brown v. Board of Education
State and local laws enacted after Reconstruction that enforced racial segregation in the South; named after a minstrel show character; lasted from the 1870s until the mid-1960s

cards.front: What were the state and local laws that enforced racial segregation in the South from the 1870s to the 1960s called?
cards.back: Jim Crow laws
National Association for the Advancement of Colored People; founded in 1909 by W.E.B. Du Bois and others; oldest and largest civil rights organization in the U.S.
National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
Southern Christian Leadership Conference; founded in 1957 by Martin Luther King Jr. and other ministers; organized nonviolent protests across the South
Southern Christian Leadership Conference
Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee; founded in 1960; organized sit-ins, Freedom Rides, and voter registration drives; leaders included John Lewis and Stokely Carmichael
Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee
Congress of Racial Equality; founded in 1942; organized the Freedom Rides in 1961 and used nonviolent direct action to fight segregation
Congress of Racial Equality

cards.front: When was Martin Luther King Jr. assassinated?
cards.back: April 4, 1968, in Memphis, Tennessee

cards.front: In which city was Martin Luther King Jr. assassinated?
cards.back: Memphis, Tennessee
